diseases.name: Tomato Late Blight
diseases.description: A serious disease caused by Phytophthora infestans that affects tomato and potato plants.
diseases.organicTreatment: Apply copper-based fungicide spray. Remove and destroy infected plant parts. Use neem oil solution as preventive.
diseases.chemicalTreatment: Apply Mancozeb or Chlorothalonil fungicide. Use systemic fungicides like Metalaxyl for severe cases.

diseases.name: Tomato Early Blight
diseases.description: Caused by Alternaria solani, this disease creates characteristic target-like spots on leaves.
diseases.organicTreatment: Apply baking soda spray (1 tbsp per gallon). Use compost tea. Apply mulch to prevent soil splash.
diseases.chemicalTreatment: Apply Chlorothalonil or Mancozeb fungicide every 7-10 days. Use Azoxystrobin for resistant strains.

diseases.name: Powdery Mildew
diseases.description: A fungal disease that appears as white powdery spots on leaves and stems.
diseases.organicTreatment: Spray milk solution (40% milk, 60% water). Apply neem oil. Use potassium bicarbonate spray.
diseases.chemicalTreatment: Apply sulfur-based fungicide. Use Myclobutanil or Trifloxystrobin for severe infections.

diseases.name: Leaf Rust
diseases.description: Caused by Puccinia fungi, creating orange-brown pustules on leaf surfaces.
diseases.organicTreatment: Remove infected leaves immediately. Apply sulfur dust. Use garlic spray as preventive.
diseases.chemicalTreatment: Apply Propiconazole or Tebuconazole fungicide. Use Triadimefon for severe outbreaks.

diseases.name: Bacterial Spot
diseases.description: Caused by Xanthomonas bacteria, creating water-soaked spots on leaves and fruit.
diseases.organicTreatment: Apply copper hydroxide spray. Use Bacillus subtilis biological control. Remove infected plants.
diseases.chemicalTreatment: Apply copper-based bactericide with Mancozeb. Use streptomycin for early-stage control.

diseases.name: Tomato Mosaic Virus
diseases.description: A viral disease causing mottled light and dark green patterns on leaves.
diseases.organicTreatment: No cure exists. Remove and destroy infected plants. Disinfect hands and tools with milk solution.
diseases.chemicalTreatment: No chemical cure available. Focus on prevention. Use virus-free certified seeds.

diseases.name: Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl
diseases.description: A viral disease transmitted by whiteflies causing severe leaf curling and yellowing.
diseases.organicTreatment: Use yellow sticky traps for whiteflies. Apply neem oil spray. Introduce natural predators like ladybugs.
diseases.chemicalTreatment: Apply Imidacloprid for whitefly control. Use Thiamethoxam as soil drench. Spray Pyriproxyfen.

diseases.name: Septoria Leaf Spot
diseases.description: A fungal disease caused by Septoria lycopersici, common in warm humid conditions.
symptoms: Small circular spots with dark borders
diseases.organicTreatment: Remove infected leaves. Apply copper fungicide. Use compost tea spray weekly.
diseases.chemicalTreatment: Apply Chlorothalonil every 7-10 days. Use Mancozeb as preventive spray.

diseases.name: Downy Mildew
diseases.description: Caused by oomycetes, this disease thrives in cool, moist conditions and attacks many crops.
symptoms: Yellow patches on upper leaf surface
diseases.organicTreatment: Apply copper-based fungicide. Improve drainage and ventilation. Use baking soda spray.
diseases.chemicalTreatment: Apply Metalaxyl or Mefenoxam. Use Phosphorous acid-based fungicides.

diseases.name: Anthracnose
diseases.description: A group of fungal diseases causing dark sunken lesions on fruits, leaves, and stems.
diseases.organicTreatment: Remove infected plant parts. Apply neem oil or copper spray. Use compost to improve soil health.
diseases.chemicalTreatment: Apply Azoxystrobin or Chlorothalonil. Use Mancozeb as a preventive measure.

diseases.name: Black Rot
diseases.description: A bacterial disease caused by Xanthomonas campestris, commonly affecting cruciferous vegetables.
symptoms: V-shaped yellow lesions on leaf edges
diseases.organicTreatment: Remove and destroy infected plants. Apply copper hydroxide spray. Practice hot water seed treatment.
diseases.chemicalTreatment: Apply copper-based bactericides. No effective chemical cure once established.

diseases.name: Cercospora Leaf Spot
diseases.description: A fungal disease causing circular spots with gray centers on many vegetable and ornamental crops.
diseases.organicTreatment: Remove infected leaves. Apply neem oil. Use copper fungicide spray.
diseases.chemicalTreatment: Apply Thiophanate-methyl or Azoxystrobin. Use Chlorothalonil as preventive spray.

diseases.name: Fusarium Wilt
diseases.description: A soil-borne fungal disease caused by Fusarium oxysporum that blocks water-conducting vessels.
symptoms: Yellowing on one side of plant
diseases.organicTreatment: Solarize soil in summer. Add Trichoderma-based bio-fungicide. Use compost to promote beneficial microbes.
diseases.chemicalTreatment: Apply Thiophanate-methyl as soil drench. Use Carbendazim for early infection. Fumigate severely infected soil.

diseases.name: Verticillium Wilt
diseases.description: A soil-borne fungal disease caused by Verticillium dahliae affecting many crops.
diseases.organicTreatment: Remove and destroy infected plants. Solarize soil. Add compost and beneficial fungi.
diseases.chemicalTreatment: No effective chemical treatment. Focus on soil fumigation and prevention.

diseases.name: Botrytis Gray Mold
diseases.description: Caused by Botrytis cinerea, this disease produces fuzzy gray mold on flowers, fruits and stems.
symptoms: Gray fuzzy mold on plant parts
diseases.organicTreatment: Remove infected parts immediately. Improve air circulation. Apply Bacillus-based biocontrol.
diseases.chemicalTreatment: Apply Iprodione or Fenhexamid fungicide. Use Boscalid for resistant strains.

diseases.name: Citrus Canker
diseases.description: A bacterial disease caused by Xanthomonas citri affecting all citrus varieties.
diseases.organicTreatment: Prune infected branches. Apply copper-based sprays. Use windbreaks to reduce bacterial spread.
diseases.chemicalTreatment: Apply copper hydroxide or copper oxychloride. Use streptomycin during bloom period.

diseases.name: Apple Scab
diseases.description: A fungal disease caused by Venturia inaequalis, one of the most common apple diseases worldwide.
diseases.organicTreatment: Apply sulfur or lime-sulfur spray. Use neem oil. Rake and destroy fallen leaves.
diseases.chemicalTreatment: Apply Captan or Myclobutanil fungicide. Use Dodine during early infection.

diseases.name: Rice Blast
diseases.description: A devastating fungal disease caused by Magnaporthe oryzae affecting rice crops worldwide.
diseases.organicTreatment: Use silicon-rich amendments. Apply Trichoderma-based bio-fungicide. Adjust nitrogen fertilization.
diseases.chemicalTreatment: Apply Tricyclazole or Isoprothiolane. Use Carbendazim as seed treatment.

diseases.name: Grape Black Rot
diseases.description: A fungal disease caused by Guignardia bidwellii that can destroy entire grape harvests.
diseases.organicTreatment: Remove mummified berries. Apply copper spray before bloom. Use sulfur-based fungicides.
diseases.chemicalTreatment: Apply Myclobutanil or Mancozeb from early shoot growth. Use Captan during bloom.

diseases.name: Corn Common Rust
diseases.description: A fungal disease caused by Puccinia sorghi producing reddish-brown pustules on corn leaves.
diseases.organicTreatment: Plant early to avoid peak spore periods. Remove infected crop residue. Use resistant hybrids.
diseases.chemicalTreatment: Apply Azoxystrobin or Propiconazole at first sign. Use Pyraclostrobin for severe infections.

diseases.name: Healthy
diseases.description: Your plant appears healthy! No signs of disease detected.
diseases.organicTreatment: No treatment needed. Continue regular care with proper watering and fertilization.
diseases.chemicalTreatment: No chemical treatment required. Maintain preventive care schedule.
